input.command:
gh release create v0.2.0 --title "v0.2.0" --notes "$(cat <<'EOF'
## What's Changed

**Major refactor: modular package structure**

Split monolithic command files and internal packages into focused, cohesive subpackages for better maintainability and developer experience.

### Commands reorganized
- `cmd/cloud/` — pull, push (cloud sync)
- `cmd/conn/` — connect, disconnect, connectors
- `cmd/ctx/` — context, knowledge
- `cmd/data/` — metrics, resolve, scrape, tier
- `cmd/email/` — list, log, replies, send, sync
- `cmd/enrich/` — enrich, save
- `cmd/hook/` — hook handlers (banner, session, sync, update)
- `cmd/seq/` — sequence, campaigns, analytics, leads, mailboxes, sends, tasks, suppress, engagement, dispatcher
- `cmd/skill/` — skills, learn, memory, install

### Internal packages decomposed
- `internal/resolve/` — split into resolve_email, resolve_linkedin, resolve_csv, helpers, types
- `internal/sequence/` — split into analytics, enrollment, helpers, mailbox, suppression, task, types
- `internal/sync/` — renamed session_summary → execution_delta, strategic_memory → execution_memory, added sync_db
- `internal/tier/` — split into db, scoring, types
- `internal/enrich/` — split into save, types
- `internal/db/` — split into migrations, schema
- `internal/assembly/` — split into helpers, types
- `internal/connector/` — split into generic_auth, generic_verify
- `internal/account/` — extracted convex_api
- `internal/update/` — extracted github
- `internal/session/` — extracted stale_skills

97 files changed, 8017 insertions, 7859 deletions. All tests passing.
EOF
)"

## What's Changed

**Refactor: modular package structure**

Split monolithic command files and internal packages into focused, cohesive subpackages.

### Commands reorganized
- `cmd/cloud/` -- pull, push
- `cmd/conn/` -- connect, disconnect, connectors
- `cmd/ctx/` -- context, knowledge
- `cmd/data/` -- metrics, resolve, scrape, tier
- `cmd/email/` -- list, log, replies, send, sync
- `cmd/enrich/` -- enrich, save
- `cmd/hook/` -- hook handlers (banner, session, sync, update)
- `cmd/seq/` -- sequence, campaigns, analytics, leads, mailboxes, sends, tasks, suppress, engagement, dispatcher
- `cmd/skill/` -- skills, learn, memory, install

### Internal packages decomposed
- `internal/resolve/` -- split into resolve_email, resolve_linkedin, resolve_csv, helpers, types
- `internal/sequence/` -- split into analytics, enrollment, helpers, mailbox, suppression, task, types
- `internal/sync/` -- renamed session_summary -> execution_delta, strategic_memory -> execution_memory, added sync_db
- `internal/tier/` -- split into db, scoring, types
- `internal/enrich/` -- split into save, types
- `internal/db/` -- split into migrations, schema
- `internal/assembly/` -- split into helpers, types
- `internal/connector/` -- split into generic_auth, generic_verify
- `internal/account/` -- extracted convex_api
- `internal/update/` -- extracted github
- `internal/session/` -- extracted stale_skills

97 files changed. All tests passing.
EOF
)"

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Nurge installer
# Usage:
#   curl -fsSL https://install.nurge.sh | bash
#   curl -fsSL https://install.nurge.sh | bash -s -- --pre
#   curl -fsSL https://install.nurge.sh | bash -s -- v0.1.0

REPO="usenurge/nurge"
INSTALL_DIR="/usr/local/bin"
BINARY="nurge"

# --- Helpers ---

info()  { printf "\033[1;34m→\033[0m %s\n" "$1"; }
ok()    { printf "\033[1;32m✓\033[0m %s\n" "$1"; }
warn()  { printf "\033[1;33m!\033[0m %s\n" "$1"; }
fail()  { printf "\033[1;31m✗\033[0m %s\n" "$1" >&2; exit 1; }

# --- Detect platform ---

detect_os() {
  case "$(uname -s)" in
    Linux*)  echo "linux" ;;
    Darwin*) echo "darwin" ;;
    *)       fail "Unsupported OS: $(uname -s)" ;;
  esac
}

detect_arch() {
  case "$(uname -m)" in
    x86_64|amd64)  echo "amd64" ;;
    arm64|aarch64) echo "arm64" ;;
    *)             fail "Unsupported architecture: $(uname -m)" ;;
  esac
}

# --- Resolve version ---

resolve_version() {
  local requested="${1:-}"
  local pre="${2:-false}"

  if [ -n "$requested" ] && [ "$requested" != "--pre" ]; then
    echo "$requested"
    return
  fi

  info "Fetching latest release..."
  local releases
  releases=$(curl -fsSL "https://api.github.com/repos/${REPO}/releases?per_page=20") || fail "Failed to fetch releases"

  local version=""
  if [ "$pre" = "true" ]; then
    version=$(echo "$releases" | grep -o '"tag_name": *"[^"]*"' | head -1 | sed 's/.*"tag_name": *"//;s/"//')
  else
    version=$(echo "$releases" | grep -o '"tag_name": *"[^"]*"' | grep -v 'alpha\|beta\|rc' | head -1 | sed 's/.*"tag_name": *"//;s/"//')
  fi

  [ -z "$version" ] && fail "No release found"
  echo "$version"
}

# --- Download & install ---

install() {
  local version="$1"
  local os="$2"
  local arch="$3"

  local archive_name="${BINARY}_${os}_${arch}"
  local ext="tar.gz"
  [ "$os" = "windows" ] && ext="zip"

  local url="https://github.com/${REPO}/releases/download/${version}/${archive_name}.${ext}"

  info "Downloading ${BINARY} ${version} (${os}/${arch})..."
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
  trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

  curl -fsSL "$url" -o "${tmpdir}/archive.${ext}" || fail "Download failed. Check that ${version} exists at https://github.com/${REPO}/releases"

  info "Extracting..."
  if [ "$ext" = "tar.gz" ]; then
    tar -xzf "${tmpdir}/archive.${ext}" -C "$tmpdir"
  else
    unzip -q "${tmpdir}/archive.${ext}" -d "$tmpdir"
  fi

  # Find the binary (may be nested in a directory)
  local bin_path
  bin_path=$(find "$tmpdir" -name "$BINARY" -type f | head -1)
  [ -z "$bin_path" ] && fail "Binary not found in archive"

  chmod +x "$bin_path"

  # Install to target directory
  if [ -w "$INSTALL_DIR" ]; then
    mv "$bin_path" "${INSTALL_DIR}/${BINARY}"
  else
    info "Need sudo to install to ${INSTALL_DIR}"
    sudo mv "$bin_path" "${INSTALL_DIR}/${BINARY}"
  fi

  ok "Installed ${BINARY} ${version} to ${INSTALL_DIR}/${BINARY}"
}

# --- Main ---

main() {
  local arg="${1:-}"
  local pre=false

  if [ "$arg" = "--pre" ]; then
    pre=true
    arg=""
  fi

  local os arch version
  os=$(detect_os)
  arch=$(detect_arch)
  version=$(resolve_version "$arg" "$pre")

  install "$version" "$os" "$arch"

  # Post-install: next steps
  echo ""
  echo "  Get started:"
  echo "    nurge my-outbound"
  echo "    cd my-outbound"
  echo ""
}

main "$@"

tag:	v0.1.4
draft:	false

author:	rejoesudarsan

url:	https://github.com/usenurge/nurgesh/releases/tag/v0.1.4
--
## What's New

### Gmail Connect via Unipile (fixes send failures)
- Migrated Gmail connection from local OAuth to Convex→Unipile hosted auth
- `nurge connect gmail` now creates Unipile account record required by `nurge send`
- Polls `/api/connection-status` to confirm end-to-end connection before reporting success
- Fixes: "No connected email account" error when sending after a successful connect

### Convex Connect Endpoints
- New `/api/connect-gmail` — initiates Unipile hosted auth link
- New `/api/connection-status` — CLI polls for connection confirmation
- New `/api/connect-success` — success page after OAuth completes
- New `/api/sync-emails` — CLI fetches emails from Convex

### Auto-Update
- `nurge update` command — self-updates binary to latest release
- Async version check on every command (2s timeout, non-blocking)
- Notifies when a new version is available

### Other
- Pending skill upgrade check on command startup
- Sync and skills command improvements

name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - "v*"

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

        with:
          go-version-file: go.mod

      - name: Run GoReleaser
        uses: goreleaser/goreleaser-action@v6
        with:
          version: latest
          args: release --clean
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.RELEASE_TOKEN }}

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Nurge installer
# Usage:
#   curl -fsSL https://install.nurge.sh | bash
#   curl -fsSL https://install.nurge.sh | bash -s -- --pre
#   curl -fsSL https://install.nurge.sh | bash -s -- v0.1.0

REPO="usenurge/nurge"
INSTALL_DIR="/usr/local/bin"
BINARY="nurge"

# --- Helpers ---

info()  { printf "\033[1;34m→\033[0m %s\n" "$1"; }
ok()    { printf "\033[1;32m✓\033[0m %s\n" "$1"; }
warn()  { printf "\033[1;33m!\033[0m %s\n" "$1"; }
fail()  { printf "\033[1;31m✗\033[0m %s\n" "$1" >&2; exit 1; }

# --- Detect platform ---

detect_os() {
  case "$(uname -s)" in
    Linux*)  echo "linux" ;;
    Darwin*) echo "darwin" ;;
    *)       fail "Unsupported OS: $(uname -s)" ;;
  esac
}

detect_arch() {
  case "$(uname -m)" in
    x86_64|amd64)  echo "amd64" ;;
    arm64|aarch64) echo "arm64" ;;
    *)             fail "Unsupported architecture: $(uname -m)" ;;
  esac
}

# --- Resolve version ---

resolve_version() {
  local requested="${1:-}"
  local pre="${2:-false}"

  fi

  local version=""
  if [ "$pre" = "true" ]; then
    version=$(echo "$releases" | grep -o '"tag_name": *"[^"]*"' | head -1 | sed 's/.*"tag_name": *"//;s/"//')
  else
    version=$(echo "$releases" | grep -o '"tag_name": *"[^"]*"' | grep -v 'alpha\|beta\|rc' | head -1 | sed 's/.*"tag_name": *"//;s/"//')
  fi

}

install() {
  local version="$1"
  local os="$2"
  local arch="$3"

  local archive_name="${BINARY}_${os}_${arch}"
  local ext="tar.gz"

  local url="https://github.com/${REPO}/releases/download/${version}/${archive_name}.${ext}"

  info "Downloading ${BINARY} ${version} (${os}/${arch})..."
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
  trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

  if [ "$ext" = "tar.gz" ]; then

  else
    unzip -q "${tmpdir}/archive.${ext}" -d "$tmpdir"
  fi

  # Find the binary (may be nested in a directory)
  local bin_path
  bin_path=$(find "$tmpdir" -name "$BINARY" -type f | head -1)
  [ -z "$bin_path" ] && fail "Binary not found in archive"

  chmod +x "$bin_path"

  # Install to target directory
  if [ -w "$INSTALL_DIR" ]; then
    mv "$bin_path" "${INSTALL_DIR}/${BINARY}"
  else
    info "Need sudo to install to ${INSTALL_DIR}"
    sudo mv "$bin_path" "${INSTALL_DIR}/${BINARY}"
  fi

  ok "Installed ${BINARY} ${version} to ${INSTALL_DIR}/${BINARY}"
}

# --- Main ---

main() {
  local arg="${1:-}"
  local pre=false

  if [ "$arg" = "--pre" ]; then
    pre=true
    arg=""
  fi

  local os arch version
  os=$(detect_os)

  echo ""
